lessons.title: Hello & ABC
lessons.topic: Greetings and the sound of A

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you say when you meet a friend?
questions.hint: You say this when you see someone for the first time today.

questions.question_text: When do we say 'hello'?
questions.hint: Think about what you do when you see your teacher in the morning.

questions.question_text: Which word means the same as 'hello'?
questions.hint: It's a short, friendly word we use to greet people.

items.definition: A greeting
Person who helps you learn
Person learning at school
A red/green fruit
A greeting you say when you meet someone
A person who helps you learn at school
A child who learns at school
A round red fruit that is healthy to eat

steps.question_text: Match the person who helps you learn: 👩‍🏫

steps.title: My First Day
steps.text: Hello! I am Emma. I am a student. This is my teacher, Miss Lee. I have an apple in my bag. Hello, teacher! Hello, friends!
steps.scene_description: happy young girl student waving hello to teacher in bright classroom

questions.question: Who is Emma?
questions.locate_text: I am a student. This is my teacher, Miss Lee.

questions.question: What does Emma have in her bag?
questions.locate_text: I have an apple in my bag.

questions.question: Does Emma say hello to her teacher?

steps.explanation: We use 'I am' to say what we are or who we are.

steps.audio_text: Hello, everyone! I am Miss Anna, your teacher. Today we have a new student. His name is Tom. Tom, please say hello to the class. Oh, Tom has a red apple for snack time!

steps.prompt: Practice saying these sentences out loud!

prompts.prompt: Say: Hello, I am a student.

questions.question_text: Which word is a greeting?
I ___ a student.
Read: 'I am a teacher.' Who is speaking?
True or False: We say 'hello' when we meet someone.
Which sentence is correct?

lessons.title: Morning & Pets
lessons.topic: Daily Greetings and sounds of B and C

steps.instruction: Let's wake up and get ready! Answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do we say when we see our friends in the morning?
questions.hint: We say this when we meet someone.

questions.question_text: Which animal says 'meow'?
questions.hint: This pet likes to sleep and catch mice.

questions.question_text: What sound does the letter B make?
questions.hint: Say 'ball' and listen to the first sound.

items.definition: The start of the day
Feeling good
A round toy (Sound B)
A small pet (Sound C)

items.hint: The time when the sun comes up
How you feel when you are happy and good
A round toy you can throw and kick

steps.title: Hello, Morning!
steps.text: Good morning! I am Ben. I am fine today. I have a ball. It is red. I have a cat. My cat is black. Hello, Cat!
steps.scene_description: Happy young boy in pajamas waving in the morning, holding a red ball, with a black cat sitting nearby in a bright sunny bedroom

steps.explanation: We use 'I have' to talk about things that belong to us.

items.sentence: He can plays with the ball.

items.question: Good ___, teacher!
items.explanation: We say 'Good morning' as a greeting

steps.audio_text: Good morning, children! My name is Miss Emily. I am fine today. Look! I have a ball. It's red. I also have a cat. Her name is Bella. Say hello to Bella!

steps.prompt: Practice speaking! Say these sentences clearly.

prompts.prompt: Say: Good morning! I am fine.
Say: I have a cat. Hello, Cat!
prompts.expected_text: I have a cat. Hello, Cat!

questions.question_text: What do we say in the morning when we meet someone?
I ___ a ball.
Which animal says 'meow'?
True or False: Ball starts with the sound /b/.
How are you? I am ___.

lessons.title: My Friend
lessons.topic: Identity and sounds of D and E

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What animal says meow?
questions.hint: This pet likes to purr and chase mice!

questions.question_text: What sound does a dog make?
questions.hint: Listen! A dog goes woof woof!

questions.question_text: Which animal is your friend and lives in your house?
questions.hint: Cats and dogs can be your special friends at home!

items.definition: A male child
A female child
Someone you like
A pet (Sound D)
Food from a bird (Sound E)

steps.title: My Friend
steps.text: I am Emma. This is my friend Dan. Dan is a boy. I have a dog. Dan has an egg. We are friends! This is a happy day for our class.
steps.scene_description: two young children smiling together with a friendly dog, outdoor playground or park setting

steps.explanation: We use 'This is my' when we want to show something that belongs to us.

items.question: ___ is my dog.
items.explanation: Use 'This' for one thing near you

items.question: This is ___ egg.
items.explanation: Use 'my' before a noun to show belonging

items.question: ___ is my boy.
items.explanation: Use 'This' to introduce someone near you

steps.audio_text: This is a dog and an egg.
Hello everyone! Look at this picture. This is my friend Dan. Dan is a boy. He has a dog. The dog's name is Buddy. Dan also has an egg for breakfast!

questions.question: Is Dan a boy or a girl?

steps.instruction: Let's review our sounds! Listen carefully and choose the right answer.

questions.question_text: Which word starts with the sound 'A'?
questions.hint: Think about the fruit that is red or green!

questions.question_text: What do you say when you meet someone?
questions.hint: We wave our hand and say this when we meet our friends!

questions.question_text: Which word starts with the sound 'B'?
questions.hint: You can throw it and catch it. It's round and bouncy!

items.definition: What you say when you meet someone
The person who helps you learn
The time when the sun comes up
Someone you like to play with
A red or green fruit
A small furry animal that says meow
An animal that says woof woof
A white or brown thing from a chicken

How you feel when you are good and happy

What you say when you meet someone
The person who helps you learn

items.question: What do you say when you meet your friend?
Who helps you learn at school?
What time is it when the sun comes up?
How do you feel when you are happy and good?
What is a red or green fruit?
What is round and you can play with it?

steps.title: Emma and Max
steps.text: Hello! I am Emma. I am a student. This is my teacher, Miss Rose. I have an apple and an egg. This is my friend Max. He is a boy. We are fine!
steps.scene_description: young girl student with female teacher in classroom, holding apple

questions.question: Who is Emma's teacher?
questions.locate_text: This is my teacher, Miss Rose.

questions.question: Is Max a boy or a girl?
What does Emma have?
questions.locate_text: I have an apple and an egg. This is my friend Max.

steps.rule_pattern: I am ___ / I have ___ / This is my ___
steps.explanation: We use 'I am' to say who we are, 'I have' to say what we own, and 'This is my' to show our things to others.

items.question: I ___ an egg.
items.explanation: Use 'have' to show what you own

items.question: This is ___ ball.
items.explanation: Use 'my' to show it belongs to you

steps.audio_text: Good morning, class! I am Miss Sarah, your teacher. Today we have a new student. This is Tom. He is a boy. Tom has a ball and a cat. Let's say hello to Tom!

questions.question: Is Tom a boy or a girl?

steps.prompt: Let's practice! Say these sentences out loud. Speak clearly and confidently!

prompts.prompt: Say: Hello! I am a student.
Say: I have a ball and a dog.
prompts.expected_text: I have a ball and a dog.

prompts.prompt: Say: This is my friend. We are fine!
prompts.expected_text: This is my friend. We are fine!

questions.question_text: I ___ a student.
What do you say when you meet someone in the morning?
I ___ a ball and a cat.
True or False: 'This is my friend' is correct.
Which word starts with the sound 'E'?
